colors.name: polar-night
colors.role: background — the sky before the show; deepens toward #080b20 as you scroll

colors.name: aurora-green
colors.role: accent, focus ring, gauge fill, the curtain's skirt (13.1:1 on polar-night)

colors.name: magenta-veil
colors.role: the hem of the curtain and the dusk end of the scroll blend (5.8:1 — accents and labels, never long body text)

colors.name: ice
colors.role: primary text and the specular highlight color (14.9:1 on polar-night)

colors.name: ice-dim
colors.role: secondary text — ice at 72% alpha composited over polar-night (8.1:1)

colors.name: horizon
colors.role: decorative ONLY (1.9:1): hairlines, the ridge glow at the bottom of the sky

colors.name: glass-fill
colors.role: glass panel body at 58% alpha over the afterglow, under blur(14px) saturate(150%)

fonts.role: variable serif for Hangul and Latin together — masthead, bulletin values, every poetic line; Korean never leaves this face
instrument voice — body copy, kickers, readings, labels; Hangul falls through it into Hahmlet

texture: Three skies stacked and never all visible at once: a WebGL fragment shader (3-octave value-noise fbm, domain-warped into ridged curtains, hashed twinkling stars, per-frame dither against banding) fading in over an always-painted CSS gradient aurora that doubles as the no-JS, no-WebGL, and context-lost fallback; behind the bulletin, a repeating multi-size radial-gradient star speckle exists mostly to give backdrop-filter: blur(14px) saturate(150%) something real to blur, so the panels read as glass instead of gray rectangles. Scrims of polar-night guarantee AA wherever text crosses the brightest curtain.

motion.name: ease-out-veil
motion.value: cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1) — reveals: fast in, long settle

motion.name: reveal
motion.value: 700ms ease-out-veil, translateY(18px) → 0, 80ms stagger per observer batch

motion.name: pointer-lerp
motion.value: 0.07 per 60fps-normalized frame — the curtains follow like weather, not a cursor

motion.name: energy attack / release
motion.value: 0.05 / 0.02 per 60fps-normalized frame — quick to notice you, slow to forget

motion.name: idle drift
motion.value: after 3.2s of pointer silence (or on touch): x 0.5+0.3·sin(0.13t), y 0.6+0.18·sin(0.21t+1.7), e 0.55+0.18·sin(0.29t)

motion.name: pointer warp
motion.value: gaussian push exp(−4.5·d²) × 0.22 aspect-units × energy — capped, so the sky bends but never tears

motion.name: scroll temperature
motion.value: --lumen-temp 0→1 over the full page (rAF-throttled); shader uniform takes the same scroll ×3, reaching deep night a third of the way down

motion.name: canvas fade-in
motion.value: 600ms ease, opacity 0 → 1 after the first WebGL frame lands

motion.name: fallback drift / sway
motion.value: 26s and 19s ease-in-out alternate — no common divisor, the CSS sky never visibly loops
